title: SACRAMENTO, CA –Today Assemblymember Gregg Hart (D-Santa Barbara) announced Assembly Bill 1866, which will address the urgent climate and public health crisis of idle oil wells. Th

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Idle oil wells in California act as a major source of methane gas emissions, groundwater contamination and air pollution.",
    "The current system for getting idle wells cleaned up in California is broken, allowing oil companies to leave defunct wells unplugged indefinitely.",
    "California's regulations have failed to reduce the state's count of idle oil wells, which have continued to grow larger.",
    "It is estimated to take between 75-100 years to plug the majority of idle oil wells in California at the current pace.",
    "Between 2018 and 2021, the number of idle oil wells in California increased from 29,000 to over 38,000.",
    "If all idle oil wells become orphan, it could cost taxpayers over $10 billion to plug and remediate these sites.",
    "AB 1866 increases the percentages of idle wells that operators need to plug each year.",
    "AB 1866 eliminates the option for operators to pay fees in lieu of submitting a management plan for idle wells.",
    "AB 1866 requires operators to prioritize plugging wells that are within 3,200 feet of communities and those that pose threats to life, health, property or natural resources."
  ]
}
```
